description: Child formula: one implementer working on one slice of a larger feature. The explorer bonds one of these per arm it identifies. Each arm owns a non-overlapping set of files and commits in its own worktree. Not meant to be invoked directly — use full-team or lite-team.

team.description: Team name from the parent molecule

arm.description: Short name for this arm (e.g. migration, api, frontend, auth-service)

files.description: File boundaries for this arm — comma-separated paths/directories. No overlap with other arms.

lint_cmd.description: Lint command appropriate for these files (e.g. 'npm run lint', 'deno lint', 'cargo clippy')

patterns.description: Space-separated paths of 1-3 existing files to use as implementation patterns.

notes.description: Explorer's notes: what to build, key constraints, interfaces with other arms

implementer_model.description: Model for the implementer. Default Sonnet works out-of-the-box with Claude Code. For ~3x cost savings at similar quality, set to gpt-4o-mini (requires Codex CLI installed) — the implementer agent auto-delegates to `codex exec` when this is set to a gpt-* model.

steps.title: [{{team}}] implement-{{arm}}: {{feature}}
steps.description: Implement {{arm}} portion of {{feature}}. Repo: {{repo}}. File boundaries: {{files}}. Lint: {{lint_cmd}}. Patterns to follow: {{patterns}}. Notes from explorer: {{notes}}. Model: {{implementer_model}}. If model is a gpt-* id, agent delegates to `codex exec` in the worktree; otherwise implements directly with Claude tools. Commits with HUSKY=0 (or repo equivalent). Does not push.
